etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "rejuvenate" + "-ed" from Latin "rejuvenare" + "-ed" (to make young again)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Make young again; having renewed refreshed and rejuvenated renewed with refreshed presence
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Make young → renewed refreshed → contemporary rejuvenated renewed and rejuvenated refreshed with renewed presence
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Make young → renewed refreshed → rejuvenated renewed → refreshed understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having rejuvenated renewed and rejuvenated refreshed; experiencing renewed refreshed and rejuvenated rejuvenation with rejuvenated presence
contemporary_usage.register: Positive; indicates renewal and refreshed revitalization with energized rejuvenation
contemporary_usage.raw_content:
